key_findings: TLS SAN expansion (T1) is the highest immediate coverage gain at lowest complexity — httpx already supports certificate extraction; SAN parsing is Controller-side; activates with HTTP lane
Nuclei template auto-selection (T2) yields 60-80% enrichment scan time reduction through HTTP fingerprinting; Oracle enrichment capacity freed for DNS/HTTP lanes
Passive DNS overlay (T3) is zero-risk coverage expansion for inactive subdomains; activates before first DNS batch via API integration only; no Oracle impact
Dynamic KRIL re-ranking (T4) improves time-to-insight by 30-50% through live signal boost of sibling subdomains; requires AWSEM priority-update hook
Graph-driven next-hop discovery (T5) is Day-3+ technique requiring populated Neo4j graph; highest intelligence density per scan slot; scheduled Neo4j pattern queries off-peak
T1 + T2 combined exceed the 30% throughput target alone; remaining techniques add coverage depth and sequencing quality
All five techniques are independently rollbackable via config flag; no restart required; zero integrity regression risk under normal operation
Parallelization is safe for T1+T3 (Day 1 simultaneous); T2+T4 (Day 2-3 simultaneous); T5 depends on T1/T3 graph population

decisions.title: Portfolio ordered by deployment readiness, not ROI
decisions.summary: T3 and T1 deploy Day 1 (lowest complexity); T2 and T4 deploy Day 2-3; T5 deploys Day 3+
decisions.rationale: Immediate gain from low-complexity techniques avoids risk of deploying multiple medium/high complexity changes simultaneously; staged rollout preserves rollback clarity

decisions.title: Universal fallback for T2 fingerprint misses
decisions.summary: Targets with fingerprint confidence < 0.80 run universal template subset (~400 of ~2000 templates)
decisions.rationale: Targeted template selection fails gracefully; universal fallback preserves detection rate for misclassified targets; false negative rate bounded

decisions.title: T4 boost cooldown at 30 minutes minimum
decisions.summary: Any subdomain that receives a KRIL boost cannot receive another boost for 30 minutes
decisions.rationale: Prevents re-ranking feedback loop; debounce ensures queue stability under repeated signal triggers from same target cluster

decisions.title: T5 triggers at 3M+ graph nodes or 72h mark
decisions.summary: Graph-driven queries scheduled only when Neo4j contains >= 3M nodes or at 72h mark, whichever comes first
decisions.rationale: Below 3M nodes, pattern query candidate yield is too sparse to justify query latency; graph density threshold ensures meaningful results

decisions.title: T3 PDNS queries async and non-blocking
decisions.summary: PDNS API queries dispatched async before batch; DNS batch does not wait for PDNS response
decisions.rationale: PDNS API latency is unpredictable; blocking DNS dispatch on PDNS response would stall the most critical pipeline lane

technique_portfolio.name: TLS SAN Subdomain Expansion
technique_portfolio.kril_integration: new SAN candidates scored at median priority

technique_portfolio.name: Nuclei Template Auto-Selection from HTTP Fingerprint
Passive DNS Overlay
technique_portfolio.oracle_impact: positive — reduces active DNS scan load

technique_portfolio.name: Dynamic KRIL Re-Ranking on Live Signal
Graph-Driven Next-Hop Discovery
technique_portfolio.oracle_impact: neutral (new candidates enter existing DNS lane)

rollout_sequence.sequential_dependency: T5 depends on T1+T3 graph population

risk_model.title: T1 DNS queue spike from SAN candidates
risk_model.mitigation: Bloom filter dedup; SAN candidate rate-limited to 10k/hr; existing ORS backpressure

risk_model.title: T2 fingerprint misclassification causes missed findings
risk_model.mitigation: Universal fallback for confidence < 0.80; detection rate ORS monitor

risk_model.title: T3 PDNS API rate limit disrupts batch timing
risk_model.mitigation: Async non-blocking PDNS queries; DNS batch not blocked

risk_model.title: T4 dynamic re-ranking feedback loop
risk_model.mitigation: 30-min boost cooldown; ORS monitor for queue reorder rate

risk_model.title: T5 Neo4j query latency blocks Controller
Combined queue depth exceeds capacity
risk_model.mitigation: All techniques share existing AWSEM infrastructure; cumulative backpressure already modeled

recommended_actions.action: Activate T3 (Passive DNS) before first DNS batch — API integration only, zero risk
Activate T1 (TLS SAN) with HTTP lane — enable httpx TLS flag + implement SAN parser at Controller
Build fingerprint-to-nuclei-template mapping table for T2 in next sprint
Add ORS signals for T1/T3 productivity metrics to Mattermost OPS channel
Implement T4 after T1/T3 stabilize; requires AWSEM priority-update hook
Schedule T5 graph queries every 4h starting when Neo4j >= 3M nodes
Add per-technique rollback config flag; single-line disable; no restart required

assumptions: A1: httpx supports --tls-grab or equivalent TLS certificate extraction in JSON output
A2: At least one PDNS API is accessible from Controller with acceptable rate limits
A3: Nuclei template library organized with technology-specific subdirectories enabling subset selection
A4: AWSEM supports priority-update on queued items without dequeue/re-enqueue
A5: Neo4j has indexed properties for pattern query fields (scanned, kril_rank_pct, relationship types)
A6: Bloom filter capacity has >= 20% headroom over current 10M baseline for SAN candidates
A7: KRIL scoring callable in-process at Controller without full re-scan
